| A832 | done | Core E2E | Use the last active xargs replacement option and preserve `-I` line-batching side effects when a later BSD `-J` controls argv placement. | Runtime probes `xargs-multiple-replacement-marker-runtime-probe-a832-20260719.log`, `xargs-mixed-replacement-marker-runtime-probe-a832-20260719.log`, and `xargs-mixed-replacement-marker-runtime-multi-a832-20260719.log` confirmed later `-I`/`-J` markers override earlier markers, and `-IOLD -JNEW` keeps per-line execution while `-J` inserts default-parsed line args at `NEW`. | A833 | done | Core E2E | Honor xargs line replacement when later batch options change input grouping: `-I... -n` splits replacement input by default tokens, while `-I... -L2` joins two logical lines into one replacement value. | Runtime probes `xargs-line-replace-maxargs-runtime-probe-a833-20260719.log` and `xargs-line-replace-batch-extra-runtime-probe-a833-20260719.log` confirmed local BSD xargs runs `-IROOT -n2` once per input token (`safe`, then `/`) and runs `-IROOT -L2` with one replacement value (`safe /`), while `-n2 -IROOT` and `-L2 -IROOT` keep the later line replacement semantics. Dist/source-before-fix probe `xargs-line-replace-batch-dist-probe-a833-before-20260719.log` showed the analyzer missed the `-IROOT -n2` root invocation and falsely denied the `-IROOT -L2` joined value. `xargs-replacement-marker.utils.ts` now records the active replacement option index, `xargs-batch-option.utils.ts` can return only later batch options, and `xargs-input-token.utils.ts` applies later `-n` / `-L` semantics only to active line replacement input. | A834 | done | Core E2E | Honor xargs max-args options that appear before line replacement, so `-n1 -IROOT` and larger pre-`-I` chunks cannot hide later root replacement invocations. | Runtime probes `xargs-pre-line-maxargs-runtime-probe-a834-20260719.log` and `xargs-pre-line-maxargs-extra-runtime-probe-a834-20260719.log` confirmed local BSD xargs chunks default-parsed tokens before applying a later `-I` marker: `-n1 -IROOT` runs `safe` and `/` separately, while `-n2 -IROOT` on three tokens runs `safe other` and `/`. Dist/source-before-fix probe `xargs-pre-line-maxargs-dist-probe-a834-before-20260719.log` reproduced false negatives for both root chunks. `xargs-batch-option.utils.ts` now exposes pre-replacement batch lookup, `xargs-command.utils.ts` passes pre/post line-replacement batch context, and `xargs-input-token.utils.ts` chunks each logical line by pre-`-I` max-args while preserving A833 post-`-I` behavior. | A836 | done | Core E2E | Suppress xargs line-replacement expansion for unterminated input quotes, because xargs errors before invoking the utility. | Runtime probe `xargs-line-replacement-unclosed-quote-runtime-probe-a836-20260719.log` confirmed local BSD xargs reports `unterminated quote` and does not invoke the utility for single- or double-quote-open root input, while balanced quoted roots still run. | A860 | done | Core E2E | Recognize macOS `/usr/bin/xcrun` as an executing developer-tool wrapper for default run mode and safe option prefixes, so `xcrun /bin/sh -c 'rm -rf /'`, `xcrun --run ...`, `xcrun --sdk macosx ...`, and `/usr/bin/xcrun ...` deny instead of slipping through as user-reviewable. | Usage probe showed local `xcrun` syntax is `xcrun [options] <tool name> ... arguments ...`, with default behavior finding and executing the named tool. Runtime probe `xcrun-wrapper-runtime-probe-a860-20260719.log` confirmed default, `--run`, and `--sdk macosx` launch the marker child through `/bin/sh`, while `--find`, `--help`, `--version`, and `--show-sdk-path` do not. Current dist probe `xcrun-wrapper-dist-probe-a860-before-20260719.log` reproduced false negatives for executing forms. New `xcrun-wrapper-token.utils.ts` skips executing flags, consumes `--sdk` / `--toolchain`, unwraps the first tool token in run mode, and returns no invocation for `--find`, help/version, `--show-*`, or unknown options; `shell-executable-command.utils.ts` delegates the `xcrun` branch. Runtime probe `launchctl-submit-runtime-probe-a865-complete-20260719.log` confirmed labelled submit commands launch the marker child with documented `--`, observed no-`--`, inline `-l`, stdout/stderr options, inline/next `-p /bin/sh`, and `-p` argv0 forms; it also confirmed help, missing label, no argv0 after `-p`, and the bad `-p /bin/sh -- -c ...` argv shape do not execute the shell script. Pre-fix compiled probe `launchctl-submit-dist-probe-a865-before-20260719.log` reproduced false negatives for basic, no-`--`, and `-p` submit dangerous shell forms. `launchctl-wrapper-token.utils.ts` now parses `submit`, requires `-l`, consumes `-p` / `-o` / `-e` in next-token and inline short-option forms, unwraps direct commands, and reconstructs `-p` program argv by dropping the submit argv0 token. `shell-launcher-command.utils.ts` now passes resolved executable-wrapper tokens to shell `-c` parsing so wrapper-transformed shells are parsed by actual execution argv rather than original option tokens. `auto-reviewer-rm-launchctl-wrapper.test.ts` now covers submit executing forms as denied and option/argv-failure baselines as user-reviewable. | A868 | done | Core E2E | Detect literal shell command strings executed from common interpreters launched through bash, so `python3 -c 'os.system("rm -rf /")'`, `ruby/perl -e 'system("rm -rf /")'`, and `node -e 'child_process.execSync("rm -rf /")'` deny instead of slipping through as user-reviewable. | Runtime probe `interpreter-shell-exec-runtime-probe-a868-complete-20260719.log` confirmed Python `os.system`, Python `subprocess.run(..., shell=True)`, inline Python `-cCODE`, Ruby/Perl `system` / `exec` with next and inline `-eCODE`, Node `execSync`, and Node `spawnSync(..., {shell:true})` launch marker shell strings, while missing-code forms and invalid inline Node `-eCODE` do not. Pre-fix compiled probe `interpreter-shell-exec-dist-probe-a868-before-20260719.log` reproduced false negatives for Python, Ruby, Perl, and Node literal shell-string forms. New `interpreter-shell-command.utils.ts` unwraps executable wrappers and extracts static shell strings from Python `os.system` / `os.popen` / `subprocess.*(..., shell=True)`, Ruby/Perl `system` / `exec`, and Node `exec` / `execSync` plus `spawn` / `spawnSync` only when `{shell:true}` is present. New `hosted-shell-command.utils.ts` aggregates osascript and interpreter hosted shell strings without adding more lines to `rm-command.utils.ts`, which remains at 199 lines. New `auto-reviewer-rm-interpreter-shell-exec.test.ts` covers shell-string forms as denied and missing-code / invalid inline Node forms as user-reviewable. Direct process-array execution such as `subprocess.run(["rm", "-rf", "/"])` or `execFileSync("rm", ["-rf", "/"])` is intentionally not frozen as a safe baseline and remains a separate follow-up surface. Verification passed in `agent-core-interpreter-shell-exec-a868-focused-3-20260719-141351.log` (1 file / 2 tests), `agent-core-interpreter-shell-exec-a868-related-20260719-141509.log` (8 files / 20 tests), `agent-core-build-a868-20260719-141519.log`, `interpreter-shell-exec-dist-probe-a868-fixed-20260719.log`, `agent-core-auto-reviewer-suite-a868-20260719-141550.log` (226 files / 698 tests), and `agent-core-full-test-a868-20260719-141609.log` (323 files / 1971 tests). Source line counts remain within the 200-line ceiling: `interpreter-shell-command.utils.ts` 111, `hosted-shell-command.utils.ts` 9, and `rm-command.utils.ts` 199. |

## Notes

- Project-specific agent routing source: `project-skills/svton-agent-routing/SKILL.md`.
- CodeGraph is installed but this checkout is not initialized, so this run uses manual scoped source graphing.
- Invest result ranked sandbox enforcement as P0 because `AgentRuntime` creates `sandboxProfile`, while shell/git tools still execute through raw `platform.process.exec`.
- Deferred: provider streaming HTTP abstraction for desktop webview/CORS requires a broader provider transport design because current `IHttpClient` is request/response and does not model SSE streams.
- PR compare URL: `https://github.com/751848178/svton/compare/master...codex/agent-audit-repair-s028?expand=1`.
- 2026-07-14 continuation: scoped to non-Devpilot AI agent code only; `docs/devpilot/external-signoff-pack.md` remains unrelated dirty work and is not touched by this slice.
- Post-A900 next candidate: continue interpreter direct/shell probing for static hosted-process variants not yet modeled, while keeping dynamic Python argv/executable/shell construction, expression-bearing f-string handling, non-shell Node `shell` option strings, and `**kwargs` subprocess shell expansion deliberately unresolved.
